---
title: "Rusty Staggerbush (Lyonia ferruginea)"
slug: "lyonia-ferruginea"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621925672/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Rusty Staggerbush"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Ericales"
tax_family: "Ericaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Ericales", "Ericaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621925672/medium.jpg" alt="Rusty Staggerbush" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/swagner23">
swagner23</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341611259">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Ericales
-   **Family:** Ericaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=164987)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in dry, sandy habitats such as scrub, flatwoods, and pine barrens.

## Natural history notes

An evergreen shrub characterized by its narrow leaves with a rusty-colored underside and clusters of small, urn-shaped white to pinkish flowers. In Florida, it is found in dry, sandy habitats such as scrub, flatwoods, and pine barrens. It typically blooms in spring and is well adapted to nutrient-poor soils, often occurring as part of fire-maintained plant communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/164987-Lyonia-ferruginea)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-lyonia-ferruginea/)
